pipeline_only.note: Filing: 'On November 14, 2012, an investment bank representing Company A, a participant in the storage industry, contacted our management' (line 1282). Alex omits Company A from the spreadsheet because Company A never signed an NDA.
Filing: 'our board of directors determined that it would be beneficial to undertake a formal review of strategic alternatives' (line 1276). Process milestone not in Alex's bidder-centric scope.

pipeline_only.bidder: Balch Hill Partners / Potomac Capital Partners
pipeline_only.note: Filing: 'Balch Hill filed an amendment to its Schedule 13D, and concurrently issued a letter to our board of directors' (line 1298). Alex omits activist action from bidder rows.
Filing: 'our board of directors established a special committee' (line 1305). Process milestone not in Alex's bidder-centric scope.
Filing: 'On April 1, 2013, as directed by the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch began contacting potential acquirers' (line 1387). Process milestone not in Alex's bidder-centric scope.
Filing: 'On May 1, 2013, Company H contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch expressing an interest' (line 1442). Alex omits Company H's bidder_interest, starting from its NDA.

Filing: 'On May 30, 2013, representatives of WDC verbally indicated that WDC's indication of interest... at a price of $9.15 per share' (line 1527). Alex records only one WDC $9.15 row (May 28); pipeline has both May 28 and May 30 entries.
Filing: 'WDC was not prepared to move forward with a transaction with sTec at that time' (line ~1550). Alex omits WDC's temporary withdrawal May 31 drop.
Filing: 'On June 23, 2013, sTec entered into an agreement and plan of merger' announced June 24 (line 77). Alex omits the press release row.

alex_only.note: Alex records 'Final Round Inf Ann' on 2013-04-23. Pipeline treats the verbal IOI phase as individual proposals, not as a round announcement. No explicit informal round announcement exists in the filing; this is Alex's categorization of the IOI solicitation phase.

Alex records 'Final Round Inf' deadline spanning Apr 23 to May 3. Pipeline does not recognize a separate informal round. The filing describes individual verbal/written IOIs, not a formal 'informal round'. This is a categorization difference.

Filing: 'On May 23, 2013, representatives of Company H contacted a representative of BofA Merrill Lynch and indicated that Company H remained interested... but that Company H was not able to increase its indicated value range' (line 1502). Pipeline does not capture this as a separate drop event.

summary.alex_orphan_assessment: 3 alex orphans: 2 categorization differences, 1 filing-grounded drop (Company H May 23)

arbitration_summary.detail: Pipeline wins on 5 precise date fields (Company B interest, Company D interest, IB retention, executed, final round deadlines) and 2 final round ext dates. Alex wins on 13 bidder_type fields (pipeline export omits them) and 3 val fields (records lower bound). Both valid on 2 date approximations (Company E drop, final round ext ann). 3 fields inconclusive (Company D cash, date_p approximations).

key_findings: Pipeline has 34 rows vs Alex's 28, reflecting broader scope (3 target_sale, 1 activist_sale, 1 bid_press_release, 2 bidder_interest, 1 extra proposal, 1 extra drop not in Alex's bidder-centric rows).
25/28 (89%) of Alex's atomic rows match pipeline events by family + date + actor.

All 9 pipeline-only orphans are filing-grounded. They represent process milestones (target_sale, activist_sale, bid_press_release) and bidder events (Company A interest, Company H interest, WDC May 30 reconfirmation, WDC temporary drop) that Alex's narrower scope omits.

13 of 28 field mismatches are trivial: pipeline export does not populate the bidder_type column while Alex records 'strategic'. The underlying pipeline actors_raw.json has the correct bidder_kind=strategic.

5 date fields where pipeline is filing-correct and Alex appears to have data entry errors: Company B date_p (Feb 13 not Apr 4), Company D date_p (mid-March not Apr 4), IB retention (Mar 28 not Apr 4), executed date_p (Jun 23 not Jun 14), and final round deadlines.

Alex records 2 informal round milestones (Final Round Inf Ann, Final Round Inf) the pipeline does not track. The filing describes individual IOIs, not a formal 'informal round'. This is a categorization difference.
Alex records Company H drop on May 23 (filing-grounded: line 1502) that the pipeline omits.
Alex records val (point value) as the lower bound of range bids; pipeline omits val for range bids. Both approaches are defensible.
